description: Identifies DynamoDB tables and GSIs with over-provisioned capacity, missing or unsafe Auto Scaling, suboptimal billing mode, or non-capacity cost drivers that require separate FinOps analysis.

cost.pricing_source: Static fallback estimate. Prefer AWS Pricing MCP, AWS Pricing API, or cost_report region-specific pricing when available.
cost.note: DynamoDB capacity pricing varies by region and usage type. Separate capacity savings from storage, backups, PITR, streams, global tables, data transfer, and reserved capacity effects.

steady.recommendation: Provisioned mode with Auto Scaling may be cost-effective when volume is high enough.

spiky.recommendation: PAY_PER_REQUEST is often safer unless provisioned capacity plus Auto Scaling is explicitly modeled.

flat_zero.recommendation: Review whether the table is retired before deleting or reducing to minimum capacity.

severity_rules.name: wcu_underutilization_confirmed
severity_rules.condition: billing_mode == PROVISIONED AND avg(consumed_wcu) / provisioned_wcu < 0.20 AND p95(consumed_wcu) / provisioned_wcu < 0.50 AND throttled_write_requests == 0
severity_rules.description: Write capacity is chronically underutilized with no write throttling evidence. Reduce WCU or tune write Auto Scaling while preserving peak headroom.
severity_rules.recommendation: Set read/write capacity to recommended values and add Application Auto Scaling.

severity_rules.name: rcu_underutilization_confirmed
severity_rules.condition: billing_mode == PROVISIONED AND avg(consumed_rcu) / provisioned_rcu < 0.20 AND p95(consumed_rcu) / provisioned_rcu < 0.50 AND throttled_read_requests == 0
severity_rules.description: Read capacity is chronically underutilized with no read throttling evidence. Reduce RCU or tune read Auto Scaling while preserving peak headroom.

severity_rules.name: missing_auto_scaling
severity_rules.condition: billing_mode == PROVISIONED AND no aws_appautoscaling_target or aws_appautoscaling_policy configured for table or GSI capacity
severity_rules.description: Provisioned table or GSI has no Auto Scaling policy. This increases both waste risk and throttling risk when traffic changes.

severity_rules.name: pay_per_request_candidate_for_provisioned
severity_rules.condition: billing_mode == PAY_PER_REQUEST AND traffic_pattern == steady AND modeled_provisioned_with_autoscaling_cost < observed_on_demand_cost
severity_rules.description: On-demand table shows steady predictable traffic and may be cheaper in provisioned mode with Auto Scaling. Requires explicit cost modeling and throttling risk review before switching.

severity_rules.name: gsi_capacity_waste
severity_rules.condition: global_secondary_index is PROVISIONED AND avg(gsi_consumed_capacity) / gsi_provisioned_capacity < 0.20 AND p95 utilization < 0.50 AND throttled_requests == 0
severity_rules.description: A GSI has independently provisioned capacity that is underutilized. Analyze and right-size GSIs separately from base table capacity.

severity_rules.name: non_capacity_cost_driver
severity_rules.condition: cost_report indicates storage, backup, PITR, streams, global tables, export/import, or data transfer dominates DynamoDB spend
severity_rules.description: DynamoDB spend is not primarily driven by provisioned capacity. Capacity right-sizing will not address the main cost driver.

severity_rules.name: capacity_reduction_blocked_by_throttling
severity_rules.condition: throttled_read_requests > 0 OR throttled_write_requests > 0 OR ProvisionedThroughputExceeded events present
severity_rules.description: Throttling evidence exists. Do not reduce capacity until traffic shape, hot partitions, key design, and Auto Scaling behavior are reviewed.

severity_rules.name: reserved_capacity_context_required
severity_rules.condition: cost_report or account metadata indicates DynamoDB reserved capacity commitments
severity_rules.description: Reserved capacity can change the economics of provisioned capacity. Check coverage and utilization before switching billing modes or reducing provisioned capacity.

terraform: Require Auto Scaling targets and policies for PROVISIONED table and GSI read/write capacity unless an approved fixed-capacity exception exists.
Require owner, environment, workload_pattern, and data_class tags where possible.
Require cost modeling evidence before changing PAY_PER_REQUEST tables to PROVISIONED or PROVISIONED tables to PAY_PER_REQUEST.
monitoring: Alert on sustained low consumed/provisioned utilization for 7 or more days.
Alert on read/write throttling and ProvisionedThroughputExceeded events.
Track p95 and max consumed capacity, not only averages.
reporting: Separate provisioned capacity savings from storage, backup, PITR, stream, global table, and data transfer costs.
Report confidence based on metrics coverage, throttling evidence, GSI coverage, and cost report detail.
